name: User asks the assistant 'has the Patel quote been opened yet?'
description: Read-side question, not a write. Assistant should query the data and answer with a concrete fact, including timestamp — not redirect to /quotes.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Sent a quote two days ago, hasn't heard back. Wants to know if the customer even opened it.

preconditions: Patel quote was sent 2 days ago
Customer opened it once, 4 hours ago

steps.action: Types: 'Has Patel opened the quote yet?'
steps.expect: Assistant answers: 'Yes — opened once, about 4 hours ago. Hasn't responded.' with an inline link to the quote
Assistant offers a draft follow-up message with an action card to send (text/email per customer preference)

evaluationCriteria.check: Inline link to the quote stands out as a link, not as plain text
Answer is direct — no 'let me check' filler
Open count and timestamp match what /quotes shows; nudge message respects customer's preferred channel
Relative time ('about 4 hours ago') has a tooltip with the absolute timestamp for screen readers
